_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The UK SAF Mandate (Renewable Transport Fuel Obligations (Sustainable Aviation Fuel) Order 2024) imposes its obligations exclusively on **aviation fuel suppliers** — defined as persons who, in the course of their business, supply aviation fuel at or for delivery to places in the United Kingdom. The obligation attaches at the **duty point** for avtur and avgas, or the **point of sale** for hydrogen. Airlines, airports, and freight forwarders are not directly obligated parties under the mandate's supply-chain taxonomy.

A freight forwarder operating in the workspace's profile sits **downstream of the obligated supply chain**: the workspace neither owns aviation turbine fuel at the duty point nor directly fuels aircraft. The workspace's role is that of a **cargo intermediary and shipper-of-record** — it procures air-freight capacity from airlines and, ultimately, from fuel-supplied aircraft. The workspace is therefore not subject to registration, certificate, or buy-out obligations under the mandate's current framework.

However, the mandate's costs propagate upstream-to-downstream through **fuel surcharges and carrier pricing adjustments**. Airlines and their fuel suppliers will pass SAF compliance costs (either through physical SAF blending cost premiums or buy-out payments) into air-freight rates on UK-origin and UK-destination lanes. The workspace's cargo verticals — live events, fine art, luxury goods, film/TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian — all depend heavily on air transport (priority mode), making the workspace a **price-taker** in the SAF cost pass-through chain.

Additionally, the mandate interacts with the **UK Emissions Trading Scheme (UK ETS)**: SAF certificates may support claims for reduced emissions under UK ETS for aviation activities. The workspace should seek legal confirmation of whether any contractual arrangements under which it procures dedicated charter or block-space agreements could inadvertently create a fuel-supply nexus triggering any registration obligation — this is considered unlikely but warrants legal review given the workspace's high-value, bespoke charter operations for live events and film/TV.

*Source: Renewable Transport Fuel Obligations (Sustainable Aviation Fuel) Order 2024, Art. 3, UK Legislation, Jan 2025. https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukdsi/2024/9780348261714; SAF Mandate Compliance Guidance 2025, DfT, Jan 2025. https://assets.publishing.service.gov.uk/media/6762717b26a2d1ff182534f7/saf-mandate-compliance-guidance-2025.pdf; Norton Rose Fulbright, 2024. https://www.nortonrosefulbright.com/en/knowledge/publications/b5f9f70c/a-new-sustainable-aviation-fuel-mandate*
